footnote_definitions.1: openpilot Longitudinal Control (Alpha) is available behind a toggle; the toggle is only available in non-release branches such as `nightly-dev`.
footnote_definitions.2: Refers only to the Focus Mk4 (C519) available in Europe/China/Taiwan/Australasia, not the Focus Mk3 (C346) in North and South America/Southeast Asia.
footnote_definitions.3: See more setup details for GM.
footnote_definitions.4: 2019 Honda Civic 1.6L Diesel Sedan does not have ALC below 12mph.
footnote_definitions.5: Enabling longitudinal control (alpha) will disable all CMBS functionality, including AEB and FCW.
footnote_definitions.6: See more setup details for Nissan.
footnote_definitions.7: In the non-US market, openpilot requires the car to come equipped with EyeSight with Lane Keep Assistance.
footnote_definitions.8: Enabling longitudinal control (alpha) will disable all EyeSight functionality, including AEB, LDW, and RAB.
footnote_definitions.9: Some 2023 model years have HW4. To check which hardware type your vehicle has, look for Autopilot computer under Software -> Additional Vehicle Information on your vehicle's touchscreen. See this page for more information.
footnote_definitions.10: See more setup details for Tesla.
footnote_definitions.11: openpilot operates above 28mph for Camry 4CYL L, 4CYL LE and 4CYL SE which don't have Full-Speed Range Dynamic Radar Cruise Control.
footnote_definitions.12: The J533 harness plugs in at the CAN gateway under the dashboard, just above the steering column. More information can be found at this guide.
footnote_definitions.13: Not including the China market Kamiq, which is based on the (currently) unsupported PQ34 platform.
footnote_definitions.14: Refers only to the MQB-based European B8 Passat, not the NMS Passat in the USA/China/Mideast markets.
footnote_definitions.15: Some Škoda vehicles are equipped with heated windshields, which are known to block GPS signal needed for some comma four functionality.
footnote_definitions.16: Only available for vehicles using a gateway (J533) harness. At this time, vehicles using a camera harness are limited to using stock ACC.
footnote_definitions.17: Model-years 2022 and beyond may have a combined CAN gateway and BCM, which is supported by openpilot in software, but doesn't yet have a harness available from the comma store.
